name: Default Frontend
description: Builtin frontend workflow contract for project setup, branch planning, implementation, visual and accessibility QA, closeout, and cleanup.

node_templates.description_markdown: Project-level frontend workflow contract with explicit setup before normal branch execution.

project_metadata_defaults.standards_markdown:
- Use Hylla MCP first for repo-local code understanding; use git diff for local deltas.
- Use Context7 for framework, language, toolchain, and CSS references the repository cannot prove. Use MDN and CanIUse for browser semantics and compatibility.
- CSS should do the work. Prefer semantic HTML, layered CSS, and shared tokens over framework-specific styling indirection.
- Ship the least browser JavaScript that solves the current need. Prefer static or progressively-enhanced rendering over default hydration.
- Treat responsive behavior, keyboard navigation, screen-reader landmarks, and contrast as first-class acceptance criteria.
- Use browser-based visual verification for meaningful UI changes. Prefer Playwright MCP or an equivalent browser automation path when available.
- For semantic, high-risk, or ambiguous work, record a semi-formal reasoning certificate covering premises, evidence, trace or cases, conclusion, and unknowns.
- During project setup or template refresh decisions, compare current Hylla state with the currently installed DB template/binding state and ask the dev before running DB-mutating updates such as builtin ensure, template reapply, or similar refresh steps.
- Shared cross-client named agents should stay aligned across Codex and Claude: `orchestration-agent`, `planning-agent`, `research-agent`, `builder-agent`, `qa-proof-agent`, `qa-falsification-agent`, `closeout-agent`, and `commit-and-reingest-agent`.
- Build in small tested increments.
- Prefer the smallest concrete design that satisfies the current requirement.
- Reuse or refactor existing code when that is the best option instead of adding speculative structure.
- No implementation, cleanup, QA, parity check, visual review, or repair work should happen without an explicit task or subtask at the correct level.
- If tests, CI, or QA fail, create a new explicit fix task or subtask before repair work begins.
- If additional repair is needed after a task or subtask was already completed, create a new explicit item at that same level instead of reopening or silently reusing the completed one.
- Every phase must end with explicit pushed-and-reingested baseline confirmation or an explicit no-repo-delta record before downstream work treats the baseline as current.
- Refactor work should use the shipped `refactor-phase`, `dogfood-refactor-phase`, `refactor-task`, and `dogfood-refactor-task` contracts when that workflow is the real fit.
- For refactor and dogfood-refactor work, builders should update slice and phase descriptions with git-diff line deltas, before-and-after repo and Hylla counts, timing windows, and cleanup/security findings after QA or validation, and orchestrators should roll those metrics up to the parent phase description plus the report artifact.
- Do not add abstraction for hypothetical future variation.
- Unresolved uncertainty must become explicit coordination state such as comments, handoffs, or attention instead of optimistic completion.
- Confirmed-good build work must be committed, pushed, and refreshed into Hylla before downstream reasoning treats the graph as current.
- Comments, handoffs, and attention are distinct coordination surfaces: comments are shared discussion, handoffs are explicit next-action routing, and attention is the durable inbox substrate.
- Template-generated blockers must be completed truthfully before done.
- Use `depends_on`, `blocked_by`, and `blocked_reason` to express prerequisite ordering until richer workflow ordering rules land.
- Never use another agent's auth or `auth_context_id`, and never paste auth material into comments, handoffs, tasks, or docs.
- Orchestrators own child auth cleanup and must leave no stale sessions, leases, or coordination trash behind.

child_rules.description_template: Project-only onboarding work for template fit, Hylla decisions, metadata lock, and preparation for the first branch lane.

node_templates.description_markdown: Project-only onboarding phase that must happen before normal branch work starts.

task_metadata_defaults.objective: Bootstrap the project, confirm template fit, compare Hylla and DB state, and prepare the first branch lane.
task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: Template fit reviewed, Hylla decisions recorded, current Hylla versus DB state reviewed with the dev before any DB update, project metadata locked, and the first branch lane plus PLAN phase are explicitly queued.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Confirm standards, Hylla freshness, current DB template/binding state, and the initial workflow shape with the dev before broad implementation or DB-mutating refresh actions.

child_rules.description_template: Review whether default-frontend still matches this project's needs before normal execution begins.
Choose `structural_only`, `embeddings_only`, or `full_enrichment` with the dev.
Run the initial Hylla ingest or refresh for the intended repo state.
Confirm the Hylla artifact is current to the intended git ref.
Compare current Hylla state with the currently installed DB template and binding state, then ask the dev before applying any DB-mutating update or refresh.
Lock project metadata, standards, auth hygiene expectations, and coordination rules before normal execution.

child_rules.title_template: CREATE OR CONFIRM FIRST BRANCH LANE
child_rules.description_template: Create or confirm the first branch lane and its working agreement before broad implementation starts.

child_rules.title_template: CREATE OR CONFIRM FIRST PLAN PHASE
child_rules.description_template: Queue the first branch lane so its PLAN phase is explicit before broad implementation starts.
Confirm this phase ended with either a pushed-and-reingested baseline update or an explicit no-repo-delta record. No downstream phase should rely on unstated baseline freshness.

node_templates.description_markdown: A branch/work lane that auto-generates the normal PLAN -> BUILD -> CLOSEOUT -> BRANCH CLEANUP phases.

child_rules.description_template: Planning phase for current-state understanding, task shaping, design intent, and validation design before BUILD starts.
Implementation phase for concrete `build-task`, `refactor-task`, `dogfood-refactor-task`, nested `refactor-phase`, and nested `dogfood-refactor-phase` work when those contracts fit the real change.
Verification, review, and readiness phase after BUILD.
Final cleanup phase for branch/work retirement once closeout is truly done.

node_templates.description_markdown: Explicit planning phase that should exist before broad implementation begins. Planning should ground the work in committed repo evidence, test the intended UX, and shape the validation path before BUILD starts.

task_metadata_defaults.objective: Understand current state, gather repo-local and external evidence, explore design intent, and shape the executable task tree before BUILD begins.
task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: Current state is understood, scope is confirmed, design intent is documented, implementation tasks exist before work starts, validation is defined, prerequisites are explicit, no hidden work remains outside explicit tasks, and semantic or high-risk claims are supported by written evidence plus explicit unknowns.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Use Hylla first for repo-local behavior, Context7 for framework and tool docs, and MDN/CanIUse for browser semantics and compatibility. Identify proof-oriented and falsification-oriented QA expectations, visual verification, and accessibility checks before BUILD.

child_rules.description_template: Have `research-agent` trace the relevant components, styles, assets, tokens, and reuse candidates through Hylla before local implementation begins.
Have `research-agent` gather the external documentation needed for semantics the repository itself cannot prove: framework APIs, browser features, CSS behavior, and compatibility constraints.
Explore design intent, interaction states, and layout tradeoffs before BUILD starts. Record the intended visual direction and open design unknowns.
Confirm the actual scope and intended outcome before implementation.
Create the detailed build-task and supporting task tree for the upcoming BUILD phase so the work is decomposable, verifiable, incremental, and assignable.
Define how the work will be validated before handoff and closeout, including proof-oriented QA, falsification-oriented QA, visual verification, accessibility checks, and counterexample traces.
Plan or confirm branch-lane basics, local workflow assumptions, and any required tooling before BUILD begins.
Define closeout, branch cleanup, and coordination expectations up front.

Confirm this phase ended with either a pushed-and-reingested baseline update or an explicit no-repo-delta record. If planning created or changed tracked artifacts, that baseline must be pushed and refreshed before downstream work relies on it.

node_templates.description_markdown: Implementation phase where concrete `build-task`, `refactor-task`, `dogfood-refactor-task`, nested `refactor-phase`, and nested `dogfood-refactor-phase` work should live when they fit the actual change. Builder work should stay incremental, minimize browser-side complexity, and preserve accessibility and visual consistency.

task_metadata_defaults.objective: Implement the planned changes using explicit build-task, refactor-task, or dogfood-refactor-task items, nested refactor phases when needed, and the smallest justified design.
task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: Planned build tasks are completed truthfully, semantic claims match the final code, browser-side complexity stays justified, accessibility baseline is preserved, no hidden work happened outside explicit tasks, and all generated QA blockers are resolved.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Run the planned project-specific checks, verify visible output, reconcile semantic claims against final code, create explicit fix work before repairing any failing tests or CI, and keep the implementation incremental and reviewable before handing work to closeout.

child_rules.description_template: Confirm this phase ended with either a pushed-and-reingested baseline update or an explicit no-repo-delta record. If the phase produced a new baseline, it must be pushed and refreshed into Hylla before downstream work relies on it.

node_templates.description_markdown: Concrete implementation task that auto-generates distinct proof-oriented and falsification-oriented QA subtasks, visual QA, accessibility review, plus a commit-push-and-reingest closeout step.

task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: The planned change is implemented truthfully, semantic claims match the final code, no hidden work happened outside explicit tasks, generated QA blockers are resolved, and confirmed-good work is committed, pushed, and refreshed into Hylla.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Run the project's frontend checks, verify rendered output, verify accessibility, run proof-oriented and falsification-oriented QA, create an explicit fix task before repairing any failing tests or CI, then commit, push, and refresh Hylla before handing the task to closeout.

child_rules.description_template: Have `qa-proof-agent` verify evidence completeness, trace coherence, claimed behavior support, component contract fit, and justified simplicity for the parent build task.

Have `qa-falsification-agent` try to falsify the conclusion through counterexamples, unnecessary browser complexity, hidden layout regressions, accessibility gaps, and contract mismatches for the parent build task.
Verify rendered output against the intended design, including breakpoint behavior, typography, spacing, state changes, and obvious visual regressions.
Audit semantic structure, keyboard navigation, focus handling, landmark roles, and contrast for the parent build task.

Have `commit-and-reingest-agent` commit confirmed-good work, push the baseline that downstream tooling should rely on, trigger Hylla refresh, wait for the artifact to catch up to that commit, and record the resulting freshness evidence for downstream reasoning.

node_templates.description_markdown: Refactor phase that layers on top of the host frontend workflow instead of replacing it. Use Hylla-first committed understanding, break the work into explicit subphases and slice tasks, preserve parity after each incremental slice, and keep metrics truthful in slice task descriptions plus phase rollups without weakening visual or accessibility standards.

task_metadata_defaults.objective: Break refactor work into explicit subphases and slice tasks that preserve behavior, improve structure, and stay grounded in committed Hylla evidence.
task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: The refactor phase is decomposed into explicit subphases or slice tasks, each slice preserved parity after QA, visual, accessibility, and in-action validation, each confirmed slice was committed, pushed, and reingested, and phase metrics are rolled up truthfully.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Use Hylla first to understand the existing shape, preserve frontend semantics, visual consistency, and accessibility expectations, confirm parity in real execution after each slice, create explicit fix work before any repair, and keep slice and phase metrics current.

child_rules.description_template: Capture the current committed shape through Hylla before edits begin, including what exists today, reuse opportunities, coupling to reduce, and parity expectations to preserve.
Break the refactor into explicit nested phases and slice tasks that are small enough to verify incrementally, with truthful prerequisite ordering and no hidden work.

child_rules.title_template: REFACTOR METRICS BASELINE AND REPORT PATH
child_rules.description_template: Define how slice and phase metrics will be captured in task and phase descriptions plus the orchestrator report artifact, including git-diff added/removed/net lines, tracked-source line counts before and after, touched files/packages, Hylla node/block/orphan counts before and after, timing windows, ingest elapsed and cost when available, cleanup/security findings, and rollup totals.
Define how each incremental slice will prove parity after QA, visual QA, accessibility checks, and real in-action validation before slice closeout.
Confirm this refactor phase ended with a pushed-and-reingested baseline update or an explicit no-repo-delta record. No later refactor slice should rely on stale graph state.

Roll slice-level metrics up into the parent phase description and the orchestrator report artifact, making sure builder-reported git-diff and before/after repo/Hylla numbers reconcile with the pushed-and-reingested baseline.

node_templates.description_markdown: Dogfood refactor phase that keeps the normal refactor contract and adds mandatory testing against the dev version plus explicit confirmation that the locally used version was updated before the slice or phase is considered complete. Metrics should stay truthful in slice task descriptions plus phase rollups.

task_metadata_defaults.objective: Break dogfood refactor work into explicit subphases and slice tasks that preserve behavior, validate against the dev build, and keep the used local version current.
task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: The dogfood refactor phase is decomposed into explicit subphases or slice tasks, each slice preserved behavior after QA, visual, accessibility, and dev-version validation, each confirmed slice was committed, pushed, and reingested, the locally used version update was confirmed, and phase metrics are rolled up truthfully.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Use Hylla first, preserve frontend semantics plus visual and accessibility expectations, require testing against the dev version after each slice, require explicit follow-up tasks before any repair, require confirmation that the locally used version was updated, and keep slice and phase metrics current.

child_rules.description_template: Capture the current committed shape through Hylla before edits begin, including what exists today, reuse opportunities, coupling to reduce, and the behavior the dogfood refactor must preserve.
Break the dogfood refactor into explicit nested phases and slice tasks that are small enough to verify incrementally, with truthful prerequisite ordering and no hidden work.

child_rules.title_template: REFACTOR METRICS BASELINE AND REPORT PATH
child_rules.description_template: Define how slice and phase metrics will be captured in task and phase descriptions plus the orchestrator report artifact, including git-diff added/removed/net lines, tracked-source line counts before and after, touched files/packages, Hylla node/block/orphan counts before and after, timing windows, ingest elapsed and cost when available, cleanup/security findings, and rollup totals.
Define how each incremental slice will be tested against the dev version after QA, visual QA, and accessibility checks and how local used-version updates will be confirmed before slice closeout.
Confirm this dogfood refactor phase ended with a pushed-and-reingested baseline update or an explicit no-repo-delta record. No later dogfood slice should rely on stale graph state.

Roll slice-level metrics up into the parent phase description and the orchestrator report artifact, making sure builder-reported git-diff and before/after repo/Hylla numbers reconcile with the pushed-and-reingested baseline.
Confirm the locally used dogfood version was updated to the intended dev build before this phase is treated as complete.

node_templates.description_markdown: Incremental refactor slice that layers on top of the normal frontend conventions. Each slice must start from Hylla-backed understanding, preserve parity after QA, visual QA, accessibility review, and real in-action validation, update its task description with truthful metrics, and then commit, push, and reingest before downstream work relies on the result.

task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: The refactor slice is implemented truthfully, host frontend conventions still hold, parity is confirmed in real execution after QA, visual, and accessibility validation, metrics are recorded truthfully in the task description and phase rollup, and the confirmed slice is committed, pushed, and refreshed into Hylla.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Use Hylla to understand the current shape before edits, keep the slice narrow enough to validate incrementally, create explicit fix work before any repair, confirm parity in action after proof, falsification, visual, and accessibility checks, then commit, push, and refresh Hylla.

child_rules.description_template: Have `qa-proof-agent` verify evidence completeness, trace coherence, claimed behavior support, component contract fit, and justified simplicity for the parent refactor task.

Have `qa-falsification-agent` try to falsify the conclusion through counterexamples, hidden layout regressions, accessibility gaps, unnecessary browser complexity, and contract mismatches for the parent refactor task.
Verify the refactored slice preserves the intended visual result, including breakpoint behavior, typography, spacing, and state changes.
Audit semantic structure, keyboard navigation, focus handling, landmark roles, and contrast for the parent refactor task.
Test the refactored slice in the real execution path after QA, visual, and accessibility checks and confirm it still behaves like the pre-refactor baseline before closeout.

Have `commit-and-reingest-agent` commit confirmed-good slice work, push the baseline that downstream tooling should rely on, trigger Hylla refresh, wait for the artifact to catch up to that commit, and record the resulting freshness evidence for downstream reasoning.

After QA, visual, accessibility, and parity validation, update the task description with truthful slice metrics: git-diff added/removed/net lines, tracked-source line counts before and after, touched files/packages, Hylla node/block/orphan counts before and after, active/wait/ingest timing windows, ingest elapsed and cost when available, cleanup/security findings, and final post-ingest totals; then hand the rollup to the orchestrator for the parent phase description and report artifact.

node_templates.description_markdown: Incremental dogfood refactor slice that keeps the normal refactor contract and adds mandatory testing against the dev version plus explicit confirmation that the locally used version was updated before the slice is considered complete. The slice task description should carry the truthful metrics rollup.

task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: The dogfood refactor slice is implemented truthfully, host frontend conventions still hold, the dev version was tested after QA, visual, and accessibility checks, the locally used version update was confirmed, metrics are recorded truthfully in the task description and phase rollup, and the confirmed slice is committed, pushed, and refreshed into Hylla.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Use Hylla to understand the current shape before edits, keep the slice narrow enough to validate incrementally, create explicit fix work before any repair, test against the dev version after proof, falsification, visual, and accessibility checks, confirm the locally used version was updated, then commit, push, and refresh Hylla.

child_rules.description_template: Have `qa-proof-agent` verify evidence completeness, trace coherence, claimed behavior support, component contract fit, and justified simplicity for the parent dogfood refactor task.

Have `qa-falsification-agent` try to falsify the conclusion through counterexamples, hidden layout regressions, accessibility gaps, unnecessary browser complexity, and contract mismatches for the parent dogfood refactor task.
Verify the refactored slice preserves the intended visual result, including breakpoint behavior, typography, spacing, and state changes.
Audit semantic structure, keyboard navigation, focus handling, landmark roles, and contrast for the parent dogfood refactor task.
Test the refactored slice against the dev version after proof, falsification, visual, and accessibility checks and confirm it still behaves like the pre-refactor baseline before closeout.
Confirm the locally used dogfood version was updated to the intended dev build before this slice is treated as complete.

Have `commit-and-reingest-agent` commit confirmed-good slice work, push the baseline that downstream tooling should rely on, trigger Hylla refresh, wait for the artifact to catch up to that commit, and record the resulting freshness evidence for downstream reasoning.

After QA, visual, accessibility, and dev-version validation, update the task description with truthful slice metrics: git-diff added/removed/net lines, tracked-source line counts before and after, touched files/packages, Hylla node/block/orphan counts before and after, active/wait/ingest timing windows, ingest elapsed and cost when available, cleanup/security findings, the dev-version result, and final post-ingest totals; then hand the rollup to the orchestrator for the parent phase description and report artifact.

node_templates.description_markdown: Post-build verification and readiness phase. Closeout should reconcile final code, final Hylla state, proof-oriented QA findings, falsification-oriented QA findings, and explicit readiness evidence before handoff or cleanup.

task_metadata_defaults.objective: Verify the completed build work, attach evidence, reconcile reasoning with final code state, and confirm readiness for push, PR, or handoff.
task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: Build gates are green, Hylla is current to final git state, review and testing happened, semantic claims still match the final code, accessibility baseline is met, visual verification passes, unresolved risks are explicit, and the branch lane is ready for cleanup.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Use the completed work plus Hylla-backed understanding to run proof-oriented and falsification-oriented QA, visual verification, and accessibility verification. Reconcile reasoning against final git state and escalate remaining uncertainty instead of forcing readiness.

child_rules.description_template: Run the project's required frontend gates such as typecheck, lint, tests, and visual checks when present. Record the green result.
Create or verify the local commit that captures the completed branch work.

child_rules.title_template: HYLLA REFRESHED AND CURRENT TO GIT
child_rules.description_template: Refresh the Hylla artifact, confirm it matches the current git state, and reconcile recorded reasoning against the refreshed graph.
Have `qa-proof-agent` verify evidence completeness, trace coherence, contract coverage, accessibility support, and justified simplicity using task state plus Hylla-backed understanding.
Have `qa-falsification-agent` attempt falsification through counterexamples, visual regressions, unnecessary browser complexity, accessibility gaps, and contract mismatches.
Review the completed change with the dev before cleanup or push.
Run the final collaborative testing pass with the dev, including visual verification in the browser when relevant, before declaring readiness.
Confirm the lane is ready for push, PR, or explicit handoff only when checks, evidence, Hylla state, and risk handling all support readiness without leaving coordination trash behind.
Confirm this phase ended with either a pushed-and-reingested baseline update or an explicit no-repo-delta record. Readiness is not truthful until the final pushed baseline state is recorded.

node_templates.description_markdown: Final cleanup phase for branch/work retirement once closeout is truly complete.

task_metadata_defaults.objective: Retire the finished lane cleanly without leaving stale branches or work state behind.
task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: Branch cleanup is complete and no stale coordination rows remain.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Confirm closeout is truly done first, then verify local git and coordination state after cleanup.

child_rules.description_template: Confirm the closeout work is actually complete before branch retirement begins.
Remove the worktree or local lane checkout for the finished branch when it is no longer needed.
Remove the finished branch when it is no longer needed.
Confirm this phase ended with either a pushed-and-reingested baseline update or an explicit no-repo-delta record. Cleanup completion should leave the baseline state explicit instead of implied.
